typography.font_pairs_explanation: Cabinet Grotesk provides authority and clarity for metrics/titles (tracking-tight). IBM Plex Sans gives a functional, technical, data-heavy feel perfect for tracking dashboards and tabular data.

colors.explanation: Stark white and tint-less greys (zinc) form the base. The design relies heavily on structural grayscale for hierarchy. Signal Red (Rose-600) is used exclusively for the critical 'Tambah Checkpoint' action. Active state surfaces use sharp black (#09090B) with white text.

layout.description: Strict grid hierarchy prioritizing data correlation and density. Mobile-first stacking, expanding to a 12-column bento-style grid on desktop.
layout.spacing: Tight gaps (gap-6) between cards. Generous internal padding (p-6 to p-8) inside cards with borders to prevent text collision. Use p-0 for map card to allow full bleed.
layout.corners: Subtle roundness, max 8px border-radius (rounded-lg) for outer containers, sharper inside.
layout.desktop_grid: 12 columns. Map: col-span-12 lg:col-span-8, row-span-2. Sidebar (Manifest + Timeline): col-span-12 lg:col-span-4, stacked vertically.

header.style: Solid background (bg-white), hard bottom border (border-zinc-200). Contains Logo and Real-time Clock.

map_container.style: Full bleed inside its container with a 1px border. h-[500px] or h-full to feel like a command center screen.
map_container.markers: Use sharp, high-contrast markers. Ensure Leaflet z-index is managed correctly (z-10) to not overlap standard UI headers (z-50).

clock.style: Tabular numerals, large text (text-xl to text-2xl), high contrast. Displayed prominently in the header or top of the sidebar. Format: Jam:Menit:Detik | DD MMMM YYYY.

manifest_card.style: Solid white background, 1px subtle border, no shadow. Active manifest summary can use inverted dark mode (bg-zinc-950 text-white) to stand out clearly from the form.

checkpoint_button.style: Massive, high-contrast Signal Red CTA (bg-rose-600). Needs hover lift (-translate-y-1) and focus ring. Text must be bold and commanding.

timeline.style: Left-bordered list (border-l-2 border-zinc-200) inside a scrollable container. Nodes placed on the line use stark black or red. Items display Timestamp (bold) and GPS Coordinates/Address (muted).

animations.hover: Slight lift (-translate-y-1) and subtle shadow for cards. Color darkening for buttons.
animations.entrance: Staggered fade-up for timeline items when a new checkpoint is added.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure all text meets APCA standards. High contrast everywhere.
accessibility.testing: All interactive elements MUST include data-testid attributes (e.g., data-testid='input-nopol', data-testid='btn-checkpoint', data-testid='form-manifest').

instructions_to_main_agent: Install and setup Leaflet for React (react-leaflet, leaflet) or standard Leaflet JS for the map.
Use standard HTML/Tailwind for structural layouts to achieve the Control Room grid.
Use Shadcn UI for Form inputs (Input, Label, Form), Buttons, and Cards where applicable, but ensure border-radius is `rounded-lg` max.
Real-time clock must use a React useEffect interval to update every second without causing full page re-renders. Use Indonesian locale (id-ID) for date formatting.
Manifest form inputs must include: No Pol (Nomor Polisi), Nama Driver, Asal, Tujuan, Muatan.
Driver Checkpoint button must request `navigator.geolocation`, capture coordinates + timestamp, and prepend to the Timeline list state.
Make sure the UI text is in Indonesian as requested (e.g., 'Tambah Checkpoint', 'Manifes Perjalanan Aktif', 'Belum ada checkpoint').
Ensure all key elements have 'data-testid' for testing purposes.
